failure_modes.text: Teams keep going because of prior investment even when the future case no longer justifies recommitment.
failure_modes.source_quote: prior investment is used to justify continued exposure
failure_modes.mitigation: Evaluate only the forward case: strip past investment from the analysis and ask whether the project would be funded from scratch given today's information.

failure_modes.text: The model fails when history is treated as a reason to continue instead of as an irrecoverable fact that should be ignored for forward choice.
failure_modes.source_quote: evaluate the stock based on its future potential
failure_modes.mitigation: Make the exit criteria explicit before starting: define in advance what evidence would trigger abandonment, so the team cannot reinterpret sunk costs as commitment.

failure_modes.text: Anti-sunk-cost logic becomes reckless when it ignores switching costs, learning value, or real upside that still remains.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Anti-Sunk-Cost Logic Justifies Reckless Abandonment
failure_modes.mitigation: Check for real remaining upside before quitting: anti-sunk-cost logic should not override genuine switching costs, accumulated learning, or residual option value that still justify continuation.

premortem_questions.text: Would we start again today from current facts?
What future upside actually remains if we ignore what is already gone?
What switching cost or learning value are we using as a reason to stay, and is it real or just emotionally easier than exit?

heuristics.text: Reset the choice from current facts and future value instead of from prior investment.
heuristics.source_quote: evaluate ... based on its future potential

heuristics.text: Ask whether the team would willingly recommit today before treating continued exposure as rational.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when the question is whether we would start again today

heuristics.text: Separate irrecoverable cost from still-live options so exit and recommitment are judged on the future, not on shame.
heuristics.source_quote: sunk cost prevents this objective assessment

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on restart-from-today evaluation, anti-escalation, and future-oriented recommitment discipline.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor behavioral-econ sprawl because the raw source is stronger on reset framing and anti-legacy-justification correction.
open_questions: The third premortem item compresses source material on switching costs and learning value so the chunk stays usable across domains.

allies: The raw ally surface is strongest on first-principles-thinking, optionality, and systems-thinking as later relation material.
antagonists: The raw antagonist pressure is strongest on commitment-bias, confirmation-bias, and framing distortion, but only clean current-vocabulary ids should survive Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The source has explicit tension blocks with commitment-bias, optionality, and confirmation-bias that cleanly map into the current target-id surface.
